title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following a discussion regarding Dr. Nicholson’s intense personality and his wife Moira’s mournful appearance, Henry Basington-French becomes agitated and abruptly leaves the room. He accuses the doctor of torturing patients by withholding drug supplies to induce madness. Afterward, Frankie and Roger Basington-French discuss Dr. Nicholson’s interest in children and his upcoming absence from town.

In London, Bobby receives a secret letter from Frankie, delivered by her maid. Frankie instructs him to travel to her under a disguise, advising him to purchase chauffeur’s clothes from Harrods and grow a mustache. She tells him to bring a note from his father claiming his car is ready and suggests he stay at the Stafely Angler Arms Hotel because the local garage is full. Frankie urges Bobby to gather intelligence on the suspicious Nicholson Sanatorium, noting that Dr. Nicholson drives a blue Talbot and has shown an unusual interest in her recent accident. She also claims to have discovered the real identity of a body.

Before departing, Bobby reads a letter from his father, the vicar, which includes a postscript about a mysterious visitor: a tall man with a slight hunchback and metal-framed spectacles who was looking for him. Suspecting this man may be an enemy, Bobby takes precautions. He leaves his Fiat in St. James Square, makes several phone calls, and travels north toward Hampshire in a Bentley. Upon arriving at Merroway Manor, a driver named Hawkins delivers the car and a note from Frankie instructing Bobby to stay at the Stafely Angler Arms Hotel.
